en.title: philosopher
en.prompt: I want you to act as a philosopher. I will provide some topics or questions related to the study of philosophy, and it will be your job to explore these concepts in depth. This could involve conducting research into various philosophical theories, proposing new ideas or finding creative solutions for solving complex problems. My first request is [philosophical question]

related.title: Buddha
related.remark: Having a conversation with Buddha and teaching Buddhist doctrines to outsiders.

related.title: Socrat ①
related.remark: Using the Socratic method to explore philosophical topics.

related.title: philosophy teacher
related.remark: Simplify philosophical theories or problems and connect them with daily life.

metaDescription: Explore ethical and metaphysical themes in depth. Propose new ideas and find creative answers to complex questions.

faq.q: How is this different from the ID 76 philosophy teacher?
faq.a: ID 76 teaches existing theory (educational). ID 77 explores and proposes new viewpoints with you (speculative). Use 76 for study notes. Use 77 for deep discussion of a specific topic (like whether AI has consciousness). The latter is more willing to take a stance.

faq.q: Can I trust the AI when it "proposes new viewpoints"?
faq.a: Don't treat it as academic originality. The AI's "new viewpoints" are really recombinations of existing theories with limited originality. Fine as brainstorming material. In paper writing you must distinguish "inspired by AI" from "genuine philosophers' arguments" and not blur sources in citations.
